pages.raw_text:
Several important matters for
legislation or department rulings
are presented by G. L. Shumway
of SCOTS Bluff, Neb., chairman of
the executive committee of the
American irrigation federation, in
letter to Arthur Hooker, secre-
tary of the local board of control
of the national irrigation congress,
which will meet in Spokane Au-
gust 8 to a.. He says:


We need legislation or depart
ment rulings along the following
lines:


Sufficient appropriation for the
United States geological survey to
pay for all surveys and topography
connected with the governments
irrigation projects.


Sufficient direct appropriations
to pay for all administration of the
same while directed by the govern
ment


Appropriations through rivers
and harbor or river Improvement
bills to pay for all federal reser-
voirs, as they are useful factors in
governing stream flow.


Permitting private enterprise to
acquire for beneficial uses the wa
